intent: The SAME logical denial (httpd_t->var_t:file read) repeated 100 times, each with a distinct audit serial (as a real kernel emits). triage MUST dedup to ONE allow line, not 100. Flood control.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). For decline scenarios these MAY disagree on purpose.

oracle.narrower_than_R_by: audit2allow itself dedups (it emits ONE files_read_var_files(httpd_t) macro for all 100). Our expected.te emits the ONE raw narrow allow. The -R proves the over-broadening direction (read -> files_read_var_files grants read on the whole var_t file set with padding), but the DEDUP behavior is the headline: both -N and our output collapse 100 records to one rule.
oracle.audit2why_note: All 100 records are byte-identical except the audit serial; audit2why replays each as TeAllowable. The 100-record audit2why.txt is intentionally verbose (one block per input record) - it is the raw oracle showing the input multiplicity.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl that emits one allow per input AVC record (keying on serial or not deduping the triple) would flood the output with 100 identical `allow httpd_t var_t:file read;` lines. Correct triage dedups to ONE. audit2allow -N proves the daemon itself dedups; RuleSteward must match.
